specification.topic-statement: The OpenAI Responses API request builder serializes a canonical LLM request into the Responses API JSON format, selecting between a string and an array representation for the input field based on conversation structure.

scope.in-scope: The structural layout of the Responses API request body, the input field selection logic, system prompt placement as the "instructions" field, reasoning configuration for reasoning models, tool schema transformations for strict mode, tool choice mapping, and URL construction.
scope.boundary: How individual canonical messages are transformed into Responses API input array items is covered by the OpenAI message serialization specification.
The decision of whether to use the Responses endpoint versus the Chat Completions endpoint is covered by the OpenAI API routing specification.
The mapping from model identifier and thinking level to a specific effort string is covered by the OpenAI API routing specification.
HTTP headers are identical for both OpenAI API endpoints; the Chat request specification describes their construction.

field.name: instructions
field.note: system prompt; present only when non-null and non-empty

field.name: input
field.note: conversation input; string when single-user-text, array otherwise

field.name: max_output_tokens
field.note: present only when a positive token limit is set

field.name: stream
field.note: present and true only for streaming requests

field.name: reasoning
field.note: present only for reasoning models that have a known effort string

field.name: tools
field.note: present only when one or more tools are defined

field.name: tool_choice
field.note: present only when tools are defined

behavior.schema-transformation:
The same three-step strict mode transformation applied by the Chat Completions request builder is applied here identically: recursive removal of all "format" fields from the schema graph, replacement of the "required" array with a complete enumeration of all property keys, and addition of "additionalProperties": false.
See the OpenAI Chat request specification for the full description of this transformation.
behavior.tool-serialization:
Tool definitions for the Responses API are serialized in a flat structure, unlike the Chat Completions format which nests tool details inside a "function" wrapper object. Each tool is an object with the following fields at the top level: "type" set to "function", "name", "description", "parameters" (the transformed schema), and "strict": true.
The Responses API tool format places all fields directly on the tool object. The "name", "description", "parameters", and "strict" fields are siblings of "type", not children of a nested "function" object. This is distinct from the Chat Completions format.
Unlike the Chat Completions request builder, the Responses API builder does not include a "parallel_tool_calls" field regardless of how many tools are defined.
behavior.tool-choice-mapping: Tool choice mode mapping is identical to the Chat Completions builder: mode 0 produces "auto", mode 1 produces "none", mode 2 produces "required", and any unrecognized mode produces "auto". The "tool_choice" field is only included when at least one tool is present.
behavior.system-prompt-placement:
When the system prompt is non-null and non-empty, it is placed in a top-level "instructions" field, not embedded in the input array. When the system prompt is absent or empty, "instructions" is omitted from the request body.
The Responses API separates the system prompt from conversation history entirely. It appears as its own top-level field rather than as a role "system" element in the input array.
behavior.input-field-selection:
The "input" field takes one of two forms depending on the shape of the canonical message list:
When the canonical request contains exactly one message, that message has role "user", and it has at least one content block, the input is serialized as a plain string. All text blocks from that single message are concatenated with "\n\n" as the separator and the result becomes the "input" string value. If no text blocks are present, the input is set to an empty string.
In all other cases — multiple messages, a non-user role on the single message, or an absent content block count — the input is serialized as an array. Each message in the canonical list is expanded into one or more array items according to the Responses API message serialization rules.
When constructing the string input from a single user message, only content blocks of text type are included. Tool call and tool result blocks within that message are silently ignored, and the concatenation proceeds over text blocks only.
behavior.reasoning-configuration:
A "reasoning" object is added to the request body only when the model is designated as a reasoning model and the reasoning effort lookup returns a non-null effort string for that model and thinking level.
The reasoning object contains a single field, "effort", set to the effort string returned by the lookup.
When the model is not a reasoning model, or when the effort lookup returns null for the given model, the "reasoning" field is omitted entirely.
For certain models and thinking levels, the effort string is "none", which explicitly disables reasoning. This is distinct from the field being absent — "none" is transmitted to the API as a deliberate instruction to suppress thinking output.
behavior.max-tokens-field: When a positive maximum output token count is set on the canonical request, it is written to the field "max_output_tokens". This field name differs from the Chat Completions API, which uses "max_completion_tokens". When no limit is set the field is omitted.
behavior.streaming-configuration: For streaming requests, "stream": true is added to the request body. Unlike the Chat Completions API, no "stream_options" object is included. For non-streaming requests, the "stream" field is omitted.
behavior.url-construction: The endpoint URL is formed by appending "/v1/responses" to the configured base URL.
